title: Question 1 (Brass et al.)
statement_text: Is there a conflict collection of size 2?
context_text: A conflict collection is a set of planar graphs that cannot be simultaneously straight-line embedded on any common point set. Cardinal, Hoffmann, and Kusters [CHK15] found a conflict collection of 7,393 planar graphs on $n=35$ vertices; this paper provides 49 stacked triangulations on 11 vertices as a conflict collection, representing another step towards a (negative) answer to this question.
attributed_to: Brass, Cenek, Duncan, Efrat, Erten, Ismailescu, Kobourov, Lubiw, and Mitchell
paper_contribution: Provides a conflict collection of 49 stacked triangulations on 11 vertices (reducing the vertex count from 35 and the collection size from 7,393), further stepping towards a negative answer; also shows that no conflict collection of stacked 11-vertex triangulations has size fewer than 36.
paper_title: A Note On Universal Point Sets for Planar Graphs
